ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* User name chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Admin, User) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  "role_description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "role_level" INTEGER /* Level or hierarchy of the role within the organization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "department_head_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department head */,
  "parent_department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_parent_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_department_head_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_head_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_departments" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date and time the user joined the department */,
  "role_in_department" TEXT /* Role of the user within the department */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user within the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_types" (
  "activity_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity type */,
  "activity_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the activity type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "activity_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the activity type */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the activity */,
  "activity_result" TEXT /* Result of the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_activity_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("activity_type_id") REFERENCES activity_types ("activity_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the team */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teams_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)
